annotations.label: System F (typed lambda calculus with universal quantification)

anchor.snippet: is a typed lambda calculus that introduces, to simply typed lambda calculus , a mechanism of universal quantification over types

annotations.note: Mathlib has no formalization of System F or typed lambda calculus systems; grep for 'System F' and 'lambda calculus' found no relevant files.

annotations.label: Identity function type A→A in System F

anchor.snippet: the fact that the identity function can have any type of the form A → A would be formalized in System F

annotations.note: This is an example about System F's polymorphic typing, not a Mathlib topic; no System F infrastructure exists in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Strong normalization of System F

anchor.snippet: System F is strongly normalizing

annotations.note: Grep for 'strong normalization'/'strongly normalizing' in Mathlib returned no matches; strong normalization proofs belong to separate lambda-calculus formalization projects, not Mathlib.

annotations.label: Undecidability of type inference in System F

anchor.snippet: type inference in System F (without explicit type annotations) is undecidable

annotations.note: No System F type-inference formalization exists in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Curry–Howard correspondence for System F

anchor.snippet: System F corresponds to second-order propositional intuitionistic logic

annotations.note: Grep for 'curry-howard' found no Mathlib files; Mathlib does not formalize this correspondence as a theorem.

annotations.label: System F within the lambda cube

anchor.snippet: System F can be seen as part of the lambda cube

annotations.note: The lambda cube taxonomy is not present anywhere in Mathlib.

annotations.note: No inductive typing-judgment relation for System F exists in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Universal Bool type in System F

anchor.snippet: is the type of all functions which take as input a type α and two expressions of type α, and produce as output an expression of type α

annotations.note: This System F-internal encoding of booleans has no counterpart in Mathlib, which uses Lean's native Bool/Prop instead.

annotations.note: Church-encoded booleans are not defined in Mathlib; grep for 'church' found no hits.

annotations.note: System F-encoded logical connectives are outside Mathlib's scope, which uses native Prop-level And/Or/Not.

annotations.note: No System F Church-boolean IFTHENELSE encoding exists in Mathlib.

annotations.note: This Church-numeral ISZERO predicate has no Mathlib counterpart (Mathlib uses Nat.decEq / decidable equality directly).

annotations.note: This System F impredicative-encoding trick for existentials is not part of Mathlib, which has native Exists/Sigma instead.

anchor.snippet: Abstract structures ( S ) are created using constructors

annotations.note: This System F-level notion of abstract structures/constructors has no Mathlib formalization; grep for 'inductive family' found unrelated matches only.

annotations.note: The System F impredicative encoding of general inductive types is not present in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Natural numbers as inductive datatype N

anchor.snippet: the natural numbers can be defined as an inductive datatype N with constructors

annotations.note: Mathlib's ℕ is Lean's built-in inductive Nat, not System F's impredicative church-encoded construction described here, so this specific System F example is not formalized.

annotations.label: Typed Church numerals in System F

anchor.snippet: The terms of this type comprise a typed version of the Church numerals

annotations.note: Grep for 'church numeral' returned no matches in Mathlib; this System F-typed encoding is absent.

annotations.label: Church numeral as higher-order function

anchor.snippet: the Church numeral for n is a function that takes a function f as argument and returns the n th power of f

annotations.note: No Church-numeral formalization exists in Mathlib to state this property about.

annotations.label: Wells's undecidability of Curry-style System F type checking

anchor.snippet: by proving that type checking is undecidable for a Curry-style variant of System F

annotations.note: Wells's decidability result on Curry-style System F type checking has no Mathlib presence.

annotations.label: Undecidability of type inference (corollary of Wells's result)

anchor.snippet: Wells's result implies that type inference for System F is impossible

annotations.note: This corollary about System F type inference is not represented in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Hindley–Milner type system

anchor.snippet: does have an easy type inference algorithm and is used for many statically typed functional programming languages

annotations.note: Grep for 'hindley' found no matches in Mathlib; the Hindley–Milner type system is not formalized there.

annotations.label: Second-order polymorphic lambda calculus (F2)

anchor.snippet: the second-order polymorphic lambda calculus (F2) was discovered by Girard (1972) and independently by Reynolds (1974)

annotations.note: No file or declaration in Mathlib concerns second-order polymorphic lambda calculus.

annotations.label: Girard's representation theorem

anchor.snippet: Girard proved the representation theorem

annotations.note: Grep for 'girard' returned no hits anywhere in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Reynolds's abstraction theorem

anchor.snippet: Reynolds proved the abstraction theorem

annotations.note: Grep for 'reynolds' returned no hits anywhere in Mathlib; parametricity/abstraction theorem is unformalized.

annotations.label: Girard–Reynolds isomorphism

anchor.snippet: a Girard projection followed by a Reynolds embedding form the identity

annotations.note: This isomorphism between System F encodings and inductive types is not present in Mathlib.

annotations.label: System F ω (higher-order polymorphic lambda calculus)

anchor.snippet: System F ω or the higher-order polymorphic lambda calculus combines the first axis (polymorphism) with the second axis

annotations.note: No files matching System F or lambda-cube systems were found; F-omega is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Inductive family of kinds in System F ω

anchor.snippet: System F ω can be defined inductively on a family of systems, where induction is based on the kinds permitted in each system

annotations.note: The kind hierarchy of F-omega has no Mathlib formalization.

annotations.label: System F ω as a limit system

anchor.snippet: In the limit, we can define system

annotations.note: No Mathlib content addresses the limit system of F-omega's kind hierarchy.

annotations.label: F ω allows arbitrary-order type functions

anchor.snippet: F ω is the system which allows functions from types to types where the argument (and result) may be of any order

annotations.note: No formalization of type-level functions/kinds as in F-omega exists in Mathlib.

annotations.label: F ω lacks dependent types

anchor.snippet: System F ω does not permit mappings from values to types

annotations.note: This distinguishing property of F-omega vs. dependent type theory is not represented as a Mathlib statement.

annotations.label: System F<: (F-sub)

anchor.snippet: is an extension of system F with subtyping

annotations.note: Grep for 'subtyp' found only unrelated Mathlib files (ToAdditive, tactics); System F-sub itself is not formalized.
